target.name: Neuralink
target.founder: Elon Musk, Max Hodak, Benjamin Rapoport, Dongjin Seo, Paul Merolla, Philip Sabes, Tim Gardner, Tim Hanson, Vanessa Tolosa

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 14 same-sector + similar-valuation-tier peers in AI & Software. Range: $2.0B - $39.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

data.narrative:
Neuralink is a private-stage AI & Software company founded by Elon Musk and eight co-founders. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of 14 same-sector companies at similar valuation tiers, including both public (1) and private (8) comparables. Representative private peers include Figure AI ($39.0B), Scale AI ($29.0B), Applied Intuition ($15.0B), Skild AI ($14.0B), and Crusoe Energy ($10.0B).

The peer-median method is applicable and yields the primary valuation anchor. The median valuation across the 14-company peer set is $6.9B, with a range spanning $2.0B to $39.0B. This reflects meaningful dispersion within the AI & Software sector at similar capital stages and valuations.

The triangulated analysis, constrained by a single applicable methodology, supports a valuation range of $5.8B to $7.9B, centered on the peer median of $6.9B. The current stated valuation of $9.7B sits above this range, a gap attributable to founder-led positioning and $1.3B+ in total capital raised. Key uncertainties include the relatively wide peer dispersion ($2.0B–$39.0B), the limited methodological convergence (single method), and differences in commercialization stage across the peer set. The valuation range reflects comparable multiples but should be updated as Neuralink's clinical milestones and revenue trajectory materialize.
